title: lathyrus pratensis f. sepium
intro: Lathyrus pratensis f. sepium, also known as meadow vetchling or narrow-leaved peavine, is a perennial flowering plant of the pea family Fabaceae. This plant contains a compound called lathyrine, which has been reported to have medicinal properties, particularly in its potential to reduce inflammation and act as an antioxidant. The health benefits of Lathyrus pratensis f. sepium include its ability to help manage conditions such as arthritis and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity if consumed in excess.
